description:
You are an edge–computing engineer validating a nightly roll-out for a mixed fleet of IoT gateways.  
    A consolidated raw log already exists at  

        /home/user/iot_gateway/logs/device_events.log  

    Your job is to use REGEX-based filtering (no literal substring matching) to extract all **CRITICAL** events that happened **only on 15 Sep 2023 (UTC)** and then generate a tiny summary report.

    Deliverables you must create
    --------------------------------
    1. Make sure the directory  

           /home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/  

       exists (create it if necessary, do **not** use sudo).

    2. Inside that directory create a file named  

           critical_2023-09-15.log  

       which must contain **only** the lines that simultaneously satisfy **both** rules below, in the exact order they originally appear in *device_events.log*:

       • The line starts with the ISO-8601 date-time string “2023-09-15T”.  
       • Somewhere after the timestamp it contains the severity tag **CRITICAL:** (case-sensitive, colon included).

       Do not include a trailing blank line—just the matching log lines, each ending in the single newline character normally written by standard tools.

    3. Still inside the same directory, create a plain-text report file named  

           critical_2023-09-15.report  

       using **this exact two-line format** (no extra spaces, no extra lines):

           Total Critical Events: <N>
           Unique Devices: <M>

       • <N> is the number of lines you copied into *critical_2023-09-15.log*.  
       • <M> is the count of **distinct device IDs** that appear between the first “\[” and the matching “\]” on those copied lines (e.g. “\[device-001\]”).  
       • Ensure both lines end with ‘\n’; do **not** add any additional lines.

    Verification information (what the grader will check)
    -----------------------------------------------------
    • Directory */home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/* exists.  
    • File *critical_2023-09-15.log* exists and contains only the correct four CRITICAL lines in their original order.  
    • File *critical_2023-09-15.report* exists and matches the two-line schema shown above with the correct event and device counts.

    Hints (optional, you may use other correct solutions)
    -----------------------------------------------------
    • `grep -E` or `awk` with an anchored regular expression like `'^2023-09-15T.*CRITICAL:'` will help.  
    • `cut`, `awk`, or `sort -u` are useful for counting unique device IDs.  
    • Expect to run roughly 5–10 commands in total: make directory, inspect source log, run regex filter, write the filtered log, calculate counts, build the report, and verify your own work.

    When you have finished, the only new artifacts should be the two files specified above inside */home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/*.
truth:
Initial state before the agent starts
    -------------------------------------
    1. The directory /home/user/iot_gateway/logs/ already exists.
    2. The file /home/user/iot_gateway/logs/device_events.log already exists and contains **exactly** the nine lines below (each ending with a Unix newline, i.e. '\n'):

        2023-09-14T22:59:59Z [device-002] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0ABC: Unexpected shutdown
        2023-09-15T00:00:01Z [device-004] INFO: Boot sequence initiated
        2023-09-15T08:23:01Z [device-001] INFO: Temperature reading 22.5C
        2023-09-15T10:00:00Z [device-003] WARNING: Battery low
        2023-09-15T12:45:33Z [device-007] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0F4A: Sensor failure
        2023-09-15T13:00:00Z [device-001] CRITICAL: Error code 0x00FF: Overheating detected
        2023-09-15T15:15:15Z [device-001] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0100: Temperature threshold exceeded
        2023-09-15T23:59:59Z [device-009] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0011: Network failure
        2023-09-16T00:00:00Z [device-010] INFO: Boot sequence initiated

    Target (correct) final state after successful execution
    -------------------------------------------------------
    1. /home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/ directory exists (mode 755 or stricter).  
    2. /home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/critical_2023-09-15.log exists and its **entire** contents are:

        2023-09-15T12:45:33Z [device-007] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0F4A: Sensor failure
        2023-09-15T13:00:00Z [device-001] CRITICAL: Error code 0x00FF: Overheating detected
        2023-09-15T15:15:15Z [device-001] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0100: Temperature threshold exceeded
        2023-09-15T23:59:59Z [device-009] CRITICAL: Error code 0x0011: Network failure

       (Each of those four lines ends with a single '\n'; no additional blank line follows.)

    3. /home/user/iot_gateway/filtered/critical_2023-09-15.report exists and its **exact** contents are:

        Total Critical Events: 4
        Unique Devices: 3

       (The file has exactly two lines, each ending with '\n'; no third blank line.)

    4. No other new files or directories are created anywhere under /home/user.
name: task_000000_d0440150
